creator: freebsd@troback.com
text:
New port: security/prelude-pflogger: Prelude-PFlogger Listens at OpenBSD PF redirect logged packet, and send alerts to the Prelude Manager.

Fix: 

-- 
This message has been scanned for viruses and
dangerous content by MailScanner, and is
believed to be clean.--0HJk4bE4VujHsVSTpY0s77VTpNERVdZ5baOnoNLCToDfzDTS
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.shar"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.shar"

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	prelude-pflogger
#	prelude-pflogger/files
#	prelude-pflogger/files/prelude-pflogger.sh.in
#	prelude-pflogger/Makefile
#	prelude-pflogger/distinfo
#	prelude-pflogger/pkg-descr
#	prelude-pflogger/pkg-plist
#
echo c - prelude-pflogger
mkdir -p prelude-pflogger > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - prelude-pflogger/files
mkdir -p prelude-pflogger/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - prelude-pflogger/files/prelude-pflogger.sh.in
sed 's/^X//' >prelude-pflogger/files/prelude-pflogger.sh.in << 'fbd9f0e6fd8c58367b0a2bf224828c29'
X#!/bin/sh
X
X# PROVIDE: prelude_pflogger
X# REQUIRE: NETWORKING SERVERS
X#
X# Add the following lines to /etc/rc.conf.local or /etc/rc.conf
X# to enable this service:
X#
X# prelude_pflogger_enable (bool):   Set to NO by default.
X#                              Set it to YES to enable Prelude PFLogger.
X# prelude_pflogger_flags  (flags):  Set arguments to use with Prelude PFLogger
X#
X
X. %%RC_SUBR%%
X
Xname="prelude_pflogger"
Xrcvar=${name}_enable
X
Xcommand=%%PREFIX%%/bin/prelude-pflogger
Xpidfile=/var/run/prelude-pflogger.pid
X
Xload_rc_config $name
X
X: ${prelude_pflogger_enable="NO"}
X: ${prelude_pflogger_flags="-d -P ${pidfile}"}
X
Xrun_rc_command "$1"
fbd9f0e6fd8c58367b0a2bf224828c29
echo x - prelude-pflogger/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >prelude-pflogger/Makefile << 'b9755b75532a33164b8dca6e1b1de06f'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	prelude-pflogger
X# Date created:			19 May 2009
X# Whom:				Anders Troback <freebsd@troback.com>
X#
X# $FreeBSD:$
X
XPORTNAME=	prelude-pflogger
XPORTVERSION=	0.9.0
XCATEGORIES=	security
XMASTER_SITES=	http://www.prelude-ids.com/download/releases/%SUBDIR%/
XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	${PORTNAME} ${PORTNAME}/old
XDISTNAME=	${PORTNAME}-${PORTVERSION}-rc2
X
XMAINTAINER=	freebsd@troback.com
XCOMMENT=	Prelude-PFlogger Listens at OpenBSD PF
X
XLIB_DEPENDS+=	prelude.19:${PORTSDIR}/security/libprelude
X
XUSE_GMAKE=	yes
XGNU_CONFIGURE=	yes
XUSE_RC_SUBR=    prelude-pflogger.sh
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
b9755b75532a33164b8dca6e1b1de06f
echo x - prelude-pflogger/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >prelude-pflogger/distinfo << 'fcbcaddce4790dcae8df286849a7d69a'
XMD5 (prelude-pflogger-0.9.0-rc2.tar.gz) = 0e4941e2fe9bec6bccfdd69904bde7ee
XSHA256 (prelude-pflogger-0.9.0-rc2.tar.gz) = 748083406d3188219be502449eddcaa219b946aa8bf3c9653487dfd2e8bad919
XSIZE (prelude-pflogger-0.9.0-rc2.tar.gz) = 303763
fcbcaddce4790dcae8df286849a7d69a
echo x - prelude-pflogger/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >prelude-pflogger/pkg-descr << '36c063a6a21e186723fbfb7db2fcab6d'
XPrelude is a new innovative hybrid Intrusion Detection system designed 
Xto be very modular, distributed, rock solid and fast. 
X
XPrelude-PFlogger Listens at OpenBSD PF redirect logged packet, and 
Xsend alerts to the Prelude Manager.
X
XWWW: http://www.prelude-ids.org/
36c063a6a21e186723fbfb7db2fcab6d
echo x - prelude-pflogger/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >prelude-pflogger/pkg-plist << 'ba80bf787f394d9302529807ad8e1c4e'
Xbin/prelude-pflogger
X%%ETCDIR%%/prelude-pflogger.conf
X@dirrm %%ETCDIR%%
ba80bf787f394d9302529807ad8e1c4e
exit
